items.title: Hadi: PAS supports Parti Wawasan Negara joining Perikatan, Bersatu free to decide its future
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — PAS yesterday endorsed Parti Wawasan Negara’s bid to join Perikatan Nasional (PN), as party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia was free to decide whether it wanted to remain in the Opposition coalition. “PAS supports and accepts Parti Wawasan Negara joining PN. “PAS agrees to accept Parti Wawasan Negara into PN because PAS is open.

items.title: KLCI futures likely to remain positive next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) futures contract is likely to maintain a constructive near-term outlook, supported by easing geopolitical concerns, improving global risk sentiment, and a relatively stable domestic macroeconomic environment. IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said market participants, however, will continue to monitor developments surrounding global central banks and geopolitical negotiations for further direction. Mohd Sedek also said next week's key external event will be the Bank of Japan's policy meeting on Tuesday.

items.title: Women are pillars of a Madani nation, says PM
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said women are indispensable to Malaysia’s development as a Madani nation, urging them to anchor the country’s progress in values, character and faith. “Without the contribution of women, there is no Madani nation that we hope for, one that is fresh and flourishing,” he said at the State-level Melangkah Bersama Madani: Wanita Beriman, Wanita Bertahan Programme at the Sabah International Convention Centre, yesterday. This is Madani,” he said.

items.title: Ringgit to trade cautiously next week ahead of FOMC meeting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to be traded cautiously next week, ahead of the June 16-17 Federal Open Market Committee meeting. Markets are expected to scrutinise the United States Federal Reserve's statements and projections for the federal funds rate for the year. "Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) will also reconvene to decide their cash rate, but markets are anticipating no change in the benchmark interest rate, which currently stands at 4.35 per cent," he said.

items.title: No hydrochloric acid contamination detected in Sungai Cherating after tanker spill, says Pahang DOE
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 14 — The Pahang Department of Environment (DOE) said no acid contamination had been detected in Sungai Cherating following a hydrochloric acid spill from an overturned tanker near the Cherating Bridge yesterday. The department said the accident site was about 100 metres from the river. “Meanwhile, pH monitoring using YSI equipment in Sungai Cherating recorded a reading of 9.4, indicating that no acid contamination had occurred in the river,” the DOE said in a statement today.

items.title: Vaping among students: A growing threat that demands urgent action
items.summary: The attractive packaging, appealing flavours, aggressive online marketing, and easy accessibility of vape products have contributed significantly to their growing popularity among students. Vaping among students should not be viewed merely as a disciplinary issue. It is a public health concern that requires a whole-of-society approach involving parents, schools, health authorities, enforcement agencies, community leaders, and the media.

items.title: Media Prima journos win 9 gongs at Kelantan Media Awards
items.summary: Harian Metro stringer Hazira Ahmad Zaidi won top spot for the Best Bahasa Melayu Newspaper Article category for her report headlined "Kusta bukan sumpahan", while her New Straits Times counterpart Sharifah Mahsinah Syed Abdullah placed second in the English category for her report headlined "Gold rush in Kelantan as global prices soar". In the Best Bahasa Melayu Newspaper Special Report category, Berita Harian reporter Nor Fazlina Abdul Rahim placed third for 15 articles on illegal jetties along the Golok River. Meanwhile, New Straits Times Press photographer Nik Abdullah Nik Omar came in second in the Best Photo category for pictures on "22 Perahu Naik Darat".

items.title: Police detain lorry driver over viral Kajang SILK Highway clip showing reckless driving, obscene gesture
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police have arrested a lorry driver after a video appearing to show him driving dangerously and making an obscene gesture along the Kajang SILK Highway towards Bandar Baru Bangi went viral on TikTok. The 35-year-old local man was detained by officers from the Criminal Investigation Division at the Kajang district police headquarters at about 8.45pm yesterday to assist investigations. Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police became aware of the footage before a report was lodged at the Kajang police station at 7.15pm.

items.title: Form Two student killed in Alor Gajah crash involving Perodua Viva and tanker lorry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — A 14-year-old student was killed after the motorcycle he was riding collided with a car before he was struck by a tanker lorry in Lubok China, Melaka, police said. Alor Gajah district police chief Superintendent Ahmad Abu Bakar said preliminary investigations found that the teenager had been riding a Yamaha LC motorcycle from his home in Kampung Sungai Siput Batu Alang towards Lubok China. “The victim was confirmed dead at the scene, while the 32-year-old car driver and the 43-year-old tanker lorry driver were unhurt,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Four men arrested for suspected cable theft in Sungai Siput
items.summary: IPOH, June 14 — Four men were arrested on suspicions of stealing Telekom Malaysia cables in Persiaran Komersial Waterfront, Jalan Lintang near Sungai Siput yesterday. Sungai Siput district police chief Supt Charan a/l Ai Wan said the suspects, 31 to 50, were arrested by an anti-crime police patrol at about 5.30am. “All suspects have prior criminal and drug records, and three of them tested positive for morphine,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Schoolboy killed after collision with car, tanker lorry in Alor Gajah
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A Form Two student was killed after the motorcycle he was riding was involved in a collision with a car and a tanker lorry along Jalan Sungai Siput, Lubok China, yesterday. Alor Gajah district police chief Superintendent Ahmad Abu Bakar said preliminary investigations found that the victim was riding a Yamaha LC motorcycle from his home in Kampung Sungai Siput Batu Alang towards Lubok China. "The victim was pronounced dead at the scene, while the 32-year-old car driver and the 43-year-old tanker lorry driver were unhurt," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Sabah CM: Accuracy of media reports must not be compromised in AI era
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Media practitioners must continue to uphold the accuracy and authenticity of facts, even though the world of journalism is currently facing rapid changes driven by digital revolution, social media and artificial intelligence (AI), said Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. He said digital transformation has opened up great opportunities for the media industry, including through the emergence of new platforms for news delivery, in addition to the use of AI to assist journalists in their routine tasks. New platforms for news delivery and AI to assist journalists in their routine tasks.

items.title: Anwar: National Integrity Enculturation Strategy can strengthen culture of integrity across all segments of society
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday launched the National Integrity Enculturation Strategy (NIES) to strengthen the culture of integrity across all segments of society in a comprehensive, progressive, and systematic manner. The national strategic initiative, introduced through the Malaysian Institute of Integrity (IIM), was launched by the Prime Minister in conjunction with the 2026 National Unity Week celebrations at the Likas Sports Complex here. NIES also reinforces previous national efforts, such as the National Integrity Plan, the National Anti-Corruption Plan, and the National Anti-Corruption Strategy, by emphasising that forming a culture of integrity is a shared responsibility of the entire society.

items.title: Explainer: What is the Swiss population cap proposal and what would it mean for Europe?
items.summary: ZURICH: Switzerland will decide on Sunday on a proposal to cap the population at 10 million in a referendum some have likened to Britain's Brexit vote that could have far-reaching consequences for the economy and relations with the European Union. * That Switzerland's population, currently at 9.1 million, must not exceed 10 million before 2050. * Once the population reaches 9.5 million, the government would have to introduce stricter immigration rules, notably in asylum and family reunification.

items.title: BN to mobilise full election machinery for twin state polls, says Zambry
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 14 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will mobilise its entire election machinery to face the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state polls, its secretary-general Datuk Seri Zambry Abdul Kadir said. He said the move was aimed at securing BN victories in both state elections. Also present were state and national leaders from BN component parties Umno, MCA, MIC and PPP.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Unity pillar of national strength, must be preserved
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said the unity of Malaysia’s multi-racial, multi-religious and multi-cultural society forms a pillar of national strength and must be preserved for future generations. He said that Malaysia’s diversity represented not only a national identity but also a valuable asset. Earlier, Fadillah attended the opening ceremony of the National-Level Unity Week 2026 celebration, officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at the Likas Sports Complex in Kota Kinabalu, Sabah.

items.title: Pakatan names Arthur Chiong as first Johor poll candidate, set to defend Bukit Batu
items.summary: KULAI, June 14 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has officially named Arthur Chiong Sen Sern as the coalition’s first candidate for the upcoming Johor state election. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail made the announcement, describing Chiong as a candidate capable of retaining the seat in the state election. “Saudara Arthur (Chiong) is also the best candidate to defend the Bukit Batu seat that won in the previous state election.

items.title: Agrobank channels RM2.1m to support small businesses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than RM5 million in micro-financing has been disbursed to entrepreneurs and traders at farmers' markets nationwide through outreach initiatives by development financial institutions (DFIs). Agrobank group president and chief executive officer Datuk Tengku Ahmad Badli Shah Raja Hussin said Agrobank have contributed RM2.1 million through a series of field engagements with traders over the past three weeks. "Of that amount, Agrobank contributed RM2.1 million," he said after visiting the Bandar Tasik Permaisuri farmers' market in Cheras here yesterday.

items.title: PM: Strong federal-Sabah cooperation key to state’s progress
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The close relationship and strong cooperation between the Federal Government and the Sabah government remain a key foundation in ensuring the state continues to progress, prosper and enjoy more balanced and inclusive development. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed this confidence following a meeting with the Yang Dipertua Negeri of Sabah, Tun Musa Aman, at a dinner at Istana Seri Kinabalu, after completing a one-day working visit to Sabah yesterday. During his visit today, the Prime Minister attended several programmes, including the groundbreaking ceremony for the Sabah Southern MADANI Link (SSML) project and the launch of the National Unity Week 2026 celebrations at the national level.

items.title: Congo says number of confirmed Ebola cases rises to 710
items.summary: KINSHASA: The number of confirmed Ebola cases in Democratic Republic of Congo has increased to 710, including 149 deaths, government data showed on Saturday. The number represented the total number of confirmed cases as of Friday, according to a situation report that documented 21 new cases in the previous 24 hours. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Home Ministry: New refugee registration programme aimed at better management, security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 14 — The Home Ministry (KDN), through the Immigration Department, is implementing the Refugee Registration Document (DPP) Programme to improve refugee management and strengthen national security through the collection of verified identity and biometric data. In a statement yesterday, KDN said the programme was introduced in line with National Security Council Directive No. Their status remains subject to existing mechanisms, including third-country resettlement processes managed through the relevant channels,” the ministry said.

items.title: KLIA: Runway closed after tyre burst incident reopened, operating as normal
items.summary: SEPANG, June 14 — A runway affected by an aircraft’s burst tyre at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) reopened at 7.40 pm yesterday after receiving final safety clearance from the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM). KLIA posted on Facebook that flight operations on the runway have resumed and returned to normal. Earlier, one of its three runways was temporarily closed today after an aircraft tyre burst, and the closure was initially expected to last until 9 pm as stated in its latest Notice to Airmen (NOTAM).

items.title: Nigerian writer Chimamanda says hospital delaying son's inquest
items.summary: LAGOS: Nigerian bestselling novelist Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie on Saturday accused a Lagos hospital of attempting to obstruct an inquest into the death of her 21-month-old son at the facility six months ago. Nkanu Nnamdi died on Jan 7 at Euracare Multispecialist Hospital, where he had been taken for diagnostic tests including MRI before he was due to travel to the United States for specialised care. The post on her Instagram page was her first public comment since the death.

items.title: Johor MB ‘touched’ by Loke’s ‘sincere’ invitation, but says yet to receive formal invite to KTM Komuter launch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said he had yet to receive a formal invitation from the Transport Ministry to attend Tuesday’s launch of the KTM Komuter Selatan service, despite Transport Minister Anthony Loke publicly inviting him to join the journey during a political event. In a Facebook post late yesterday, Onn Hafiz said he had only learned through media reports that the invitation had been extended during a political event. On Friday, Loke, who is also DAP secretary-general, invited Onn Hafiz to sit beside him on the KTM Komuter Selatan service from Kulai to Johor Bahru, days after the Johor BN chairman said he would rather forgo the menteri besar post than sit at the same table with DAP.

items.title: High-school dropout to big-time crime boss, Venezuela's 'Nino Guerrero'
items.summary: CARACAS: The Tren de Aragua leader killed in a US-Venezuelan raid was a high-school dropout who lived in comfort behind bars as he transformed a prison gang into one of the most powerful and extensive criminal organisations in Latin America. Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, alias Nino Guerrero, or "child warrior", died at age 42 in a raid announced Friday by President Donald Trump and later confirmed by Venezuela. Founded in Venezuela in 2014, Tren de Aragua has been designated a terrorist organisation by the United States and is believed to be active in eight South American countries including Colombia, Peru and Chile.

items.title: Iran's late supreme leader Ali Khamenei to be buried July 9
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's former supreme leader Ali Khamenei, who ruled the country for nearly 37 years before being killed by Israeli and US air strikes on Feb 28, will be buried on July 9, state television reported Saturday. The burial in his hometown, the northeastern holy city of Mashhad, initially scheduled for March but postponed due to the war, will follow three days of funeral ceremonies in capital Teheran beginning July 4 and another in the holy city of Qom on July 7, it said. Ali Khamenei's son, Mojtaba, succeeded him as supreme leader in early March, the third since the establishment of the Islamic republic in 1979.

items.title: Mayor shot dead in southern Mexico
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: A mayor was shot dead in an area of southern Mexico where drug cartels are battling to control trafficking routes, local authorities said Saturday. Joel Bravo – the mayor of San Miguel Amatitlan, a town of nearly 7,000 residents – died in an armed attack, prosecutors in Oaxaca state said in a statement, without offering further details about the incident. Nearly 100 mayors have been assassinated in Mexico since 2006, when drug violence exploded in the country.

items.title: Onn Hafiz: Teamwork is vital for Johor's success [WATCH]
items.summary: But some believe the state government's success is driven more by personal leadership, not the strength of the executive council. Answer: The success that Johor enjoys today is the result of the close cooperation between state councillors and government officials at the federal and state levels. For Johor to succeed, we must move as a team.

items.title: No deposit, no landlord: Why some Malaysians are choosing hotels over renting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — As rental costs rise and hefty deposits put traditional housing further out of reach, some Malaysians are choosing an unlikely alternative: living in hotels for months, or even years. For some, hotel living offers a way to avoid large upfront rental costs and long-term tenancy agreements. Unlike room rentals that often involve sharing common spaces with strangers, Fareez said the hotel provided a self-contained living arrangement without the need for long-term commitments.

items.title: Trump names James McDonald as US Attorney for high-profile New York district
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said on Saturday he will appoint James McDonald to replace Jay Clayton as the US Attorney for the Southern District of New York. The Manhattan US Attorney is considered one of the most powerful prosecutors in the country. McDonald, whose previous roles include assistant US Attorney and Commodity Futures Trading Commission enforcement chief, is an attorney at Sullivan & Cromwell law firm, where Clayton also worked before taking over SDNY last year.

items.title: North Korea says 'denuclearisation' is a matter terminated irreversibly
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea said on Sunday denuclearisation is a matter that is terminated irreversibly, as it condemned recent nuclear deterrence talks between the US and South Korea. "The US and its vassal forces' meaningless rhetoric against the DPRK and cooperation in posing a nuclear threat to it can never affect the irreversible position of the DPRK as a nuclear weapons state," a spokesperson for North Korea's foreign ministry said in a statement carried by state media KCNA, using the country's official name. Last week, the US and South Korean officials discussed strengthening nuclear deterrence and readiness against North Korea's growing weapons programme at talks in Seoul under their ‌Nuclear Consultative Group (NCG).

items.title: Lebanon reports strikes on south, east as Israel issues broad evacuation warnings
items.summary: LEBANON: Lebanon reported Israeli strikes in the country's south on Saturday, as the Israeli army issued evacuation warnings for the city of Nabatieh and more than 20 other locations ahead of raids. The state-run National News Agency (NNA) said Israeli airstrikes had hit several areas, particularly around the southern city of Nabatieh. NNA also said an Israeli strike killed a local official in Rihan, the southern region of Jezzine.

items.title: Far-right demo in Italy calls for 'remigration' of foreigners
items.summary: ROME: Around three thousand far-right protesters from all over Italy gathered in Rome Saturday to call for the "remigration" of migrants back to their home countries, as a retired general launched a new far-right party. A few metres away, the European Parliament member and former general Roberto Vannacci held a meeting to formally launch his new far-right party Futuro Nazionale (FN, National Future). Vannacci is a former member of Deputy Prime Minister Matteo Salvini's anti-immigrant League party.

items.title: Burst tyre incident shuts down KLIA runway temporarily
items.summary: SEPANG, June 13 — One of the three runways at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) here, remains temporarily closed until 9 pm today following an aircraft tyre burst incident earlier. KLIA said in a Facebook post that the temporary closure has been reflected in the latest Notice to Airmen (NOTAM), while operations on the other two runways continue as normal. "Our teams are working closely with the airline and relevant stakeholders to complete recovery works and restore normal operations as safely and efficiently as possible,” the post read.

items.title: PM: Govt will consider RM5m matching grant in reviving 10 statesmen memorials
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 13 — The government will consider providing a RM5 million Matching Grant under Budget 2027 to support efforts to strengthen national identity, patriotism and national unity. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative, handled by the National Archives of Malaysia, includes efforts to revive 10 statesmen’s memorials nationwide and accelerate the digitisation of the country’s historical treasures. Anwar said the MADANI Government would also provide activity grants totalling RM10,000 to 189 Rukun Negara Secretariats in higher learning institutions (IPTs) nationwide to implement nation-building, patriotism and unity programmes among students.

items.title: Police probe viral footage of man assaulting domestic helper in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 13 — Police are probing a man for violently assaulting a female domestic helper after she was caught allegedly beating his son in viral video footage. Earlier, a man had allegedly assaulted a female domestic helper in what was a minute and 32-second long Facebook video. The man, who questioned the domestic helper for beating his son, was seen punching and slapping the domestic helper in a living room setting.

items.title: National cohesion more important than any singular agenda, says PM Anwar
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 13 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reminded Malaysians not to become caught up in narratives or actions that divide people along racial, regional and religious lines. Speaking at the National Unity Week celebration here today, he called on Malaysians to remain vigilant against factors that could undermine national unity among the country’s diverse communities. “Do not be careless with matters that can fracture unity,” he said, adding that nation-building requires patience, wisdom and mutual understanding as different communities progress at varying paces.

items.title: Hadi says Hamzah Zainudin to resume role as Opposition Leader
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin will return as Opposition Leader just two months after relinquishing the post, PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced today. Hadi also announced that Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar would remain chairman of Perikatan Nasional (PN). “PAS has also decided to maintain Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as Perikatan Nasional chairman,” Hadi said.

items.title: Two women arrested after infant’s body found outside Petaling Jaya baby hatch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Police have arrested two women in their 20s to assist investigations into the discovery of a baby’s body outside a baby hatch in Petaling Jaya yesterday. Petaling Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said officers received a report at 8.10am regarding the discovery outside the facility on Jalan Bukit Gasing. “Police have also seized several items to assist in the investigation,” he said.

items.title: Hulu Langat MP says demolition of Sungai Tekali Rohingya settlement underway, to be completed in two weeks
items.summary: HULU LANGAT, June 13 — The demolition of settlement structures housing Rohingya citizens in Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Tekali, here is expected to take two weeks, said Hulu Langat Member of Parliament Mohd Sany Hamzan. He said the process began at the back of the area, involving structures built on government reserve land and a portion of private land. “I have been informed that the landowner needs two weeks to demolish the single-story structures at the back, and not the building rumored to be a three-story flat or apartment that went viral previously,” he said when contacted today.

items.title: Hajiji says Sabah Southern Madani Link will strengthen energy security and drive development
items.summary: SIPITANG, June 13 — The Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) project will serve as a catalyst for Sabah’s economic development through the provision of a more stable, robust and high-quality electricity supply system to meet the state’s future development needs, said Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. He said the long-awaited project is not merely an electricity transmission infrastructure project but also a strategic investment that will have a major impact on the stability and sustainability of the state’s energy sector. Hajiji said the state government has charted the direction of the energy sector through the Sabah Energy Roadmap and Master Plan 2040, which is centred on three main pillars: energy security, equitable access to energy and energy sustainability.

items.title: Fahmi urges Media Council to work with social media platforms against fake news ahead of state polls
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 13 — The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has been urged to meet with social media platforms to coordinate measures against fake news and misleading content ahead of the state elections in Johor and Negeri Sembilan. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the move is vital amid growing concerns over the spread of misinformation, including the misuse of local media logos to generate fake graphics and reports on social media. Fahmi said social media platforms must also play a more proactive role by taking immediate action against user-reported fake content, especially regarding elections.

items.title: Fahmi: Pakatan Harapan must focus on its own strengths, not opponents’ weaknesses
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 13 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) should not rely on the weaknesses of its opponents but instead focus on its own strengths in facing the upcoming state elections, said PH Communications Director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He was responding to a question on PH’s confidence in facing the upcomimg state elections, particularly in Negeri Sembilan, taking into account the breakdown in political ties between Bersatu and PAS. In the 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election, Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) formed the state government after winning 31 of the 36 state seats.

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin names Parti Wawasan Negara as Reset’s political platform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Former Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin today announced that he and the Reset movement would operate under Parti Wawasan Negara, describing it as a platform for cooperation with PAS and other like-minded parties. Speaking at the Reset convention in Tanah Merah, Kelantan, the Larut MP said the party’s name had been proposed by PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang. Sinar Harian reported that Hamzah said Parti Wawasan Negara was intended to strengthen political cooperation among parties that shared similar aspirations.

items.title: Hazmat deployed after tanker overturns near Cherating Bridge, leaking hydrochloric acid into mangrove area
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — An ISO tank lorry carrying nearly 25 tonnes of hydrochloric acid overturned near Cherating Bridge in Pahang this morning, leaking the hazardous chemical into a nearby mangrove area and triggering an emergency response. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahudin Isa said the leak involved less than 1,000 litres of the acid. “It is estimated that less than 1,000 litres of acid were spilled as the leak involved the tank’s upper hatch cover,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Dr Wan Azizah urges women facing emotional distress to seek help through Talian Kasih and HEAL
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 13 — Women facing emotional distress or personal challenges are encouraged to make use of the Talian Kasih 15999 and HEAL 15555 helplines to seek assistance and support services. Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, chairman of Pertubuhan Kebajikan Amal Silaturahim Isteri-Isteri Harapan Malaysia (Kasih Malaysia), said the platforms provide women with an avenue to share their concerns, express their feelings and obtain help when dealing with various life challenges. “Make use of the support available through family institutions, Talian Kasih and the Talian HEAL, which offer social and psychosocial support.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s strong ties and political stability helping secure energy supplies
items.summary: SIPITANG, June 13 — Malaysia’s strong international relations and political stability have enabled the country to secure continued access to oil and gas supplies despite growing uncertainty arising from the global energy crisis, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said Malaysia’s prudent diplomatic approach had allowed countries such as Russia and Turkmenistan to regard Malaysia as a close and trusted partner. Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud.
